items.title: Upko’s application to join Sabah ruling coalition set for decision on June 18
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 7 — Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) is expected to decide on the United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation's (Upko) application to join the coalition as a component party on June 18. He said a key condition for Upko's admission is its commitment to GRS’ four core principles: Sabah First, Sabah Forward, Sabah Prosper and Sabah United. GRS currently comprises five component parties: Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah, Parti Bersatu Sabah, Liberal Democratic Party, Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah and Parti Cinta Sabah.

items.title: Govt approves RM267m to keep Bomba on 100pc standby ‘at all times’ for emergencies
items.summary: TELUK INTAN, June 7 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) has allocated substantial funds to ensure the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) remains fully prepared for emergencies at all times. Minister Nga Kor Ming said the ministry has approved RM267 million this year to upgrade equipment for search and rescue missions. Nga said the ministry will provide written details on the estimated costs of Jaslinda’s search and rescue mission.

items.title: Former Kedah deputy speaker shared final dinner after daughter's engagement
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Nur Anisa Mohd Jamil's last memory of her father was sharing dinner after her engagement ceremony, a day before he was found drowned today. Nur Anisa, 27, the youngest child of Mohd Jamil, said her father appeared healthy and in good spirits during her engagement ceremony yesterday. Mohd Jamil, 78, a former Pengkalan Kundor assemblyman, was found drowned in Sungai Teluk Bagan in Tandop today.

items.title: Jaslinda found note left by mountain guide during search for her
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, found a note left behind by rescuers at Kem Air Busuk on Gunung Batu Putih while search and rescue (SAR) operations were underway. Perak Fire and Rescue Department Operations Division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said Jaslinda brought the note out with her and showed it to firefighters yesterday after she was rescued. "Jaslinda only reached the area yesterday," he said.

items.title: The highway crawl begins: Slow afternoon traffic as holidaymakers return to the Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — Traffic flow on several major highways leading to Kuala Lumpur was reported to be slow as of 2pm today as motorists began returning home on the final day of the mid-year school holidays and following recent double public holidays. Meanwhile, on the Kuala Lumpur–Karak Expressway (KLK) heading towards Kuala Lumpur, slow-moving traffic was detected from Gambang to Sri Jaya and along the stretch between Lentang and Bukit Tinggi. As a proactive measure to ease congestion and improve traffic flow, highway authorities have activated Smart Lanes, which utilise emergency lanes at several strategic locations.

items.title: Perikatan on the brink? PAS to decide fate of Bersatu alliance in Monday meeting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — The future of the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition hangs in the balance as PAS will convene a high-stakes central working committee (CWC) meeting tomorrow night to determine its ongoing relationship with Bersatu. PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang confirmed the meeting today, when speaking to reporters following the launch of a book on the Iran-Israel war at the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair, Free Malaysia Today reported. We will have a meeting at night,” Hadi said, addressing mounting speculation over when the party leadership would formally decide the trajectory of the PAS-Bersatu partnership.

items.title: ‘UPSI Berduka’: Book, fund and new crisis management guide follow Gerik bus crash that killed 15 varsity students
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH, June 7 — Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) has published a book titled UPSI Berduka , documenting the tragic road accident involving its students in Gerik, Perak, as a reference and guide for future crisis management and emergency response efforts. UPSI vice-chancellor Prof Datuk Dr Md Amin Md Taff said the publication, which took nearly 10 months to complete, details the chronology of the tragedy, the seven phases of crisis management undertaken by the university in collaboration with various agencies, and personal accounts from those directly affected by the incident. He said the book serves as a comprehensive record of the experiences of victims’ families, survivors, volunteers, rescue personnel, frontline workers and other parties involved throughout the management of the tragedy.

items.title: Bankrupt at 28: Former CEO rebuilds life after business collapse
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: "I have always prayed to Allah SWT to take my life once all my debts are cleared," said Muhd Wafiq, 33, who was once a millionaire and a chief executive officer (CEO) at a young age before going bankrupt at 28 after falling victim to betrayal. Despite the hardship, he had no choice but to rebuild his life and earn a living. Today, Muhd Wafiq is still bankrupt, but his life is more stable.

items.title: OPEC+ set for fourth oil quota hike since Hormuz closure
items.summary: LONDON: OPEC+ is set to agree on Sunday a fourth increase in oil output targets in as many months, three OPEC+ sources said, even though the US war with Iran is still preventing several of the group's members from pumping more. Seven core members of OPEC+, which groups OPEC and allied producers including Russia, have increased their output quotas from April to June by almost 600,000 barrels per day. On Sunday, the seven members will likely increase targets by about 188,000 bpd from July, the sources said.

items.title: Cosmetics exports top US$5.6bil in Jan-May, Seoul's top consumer export item
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korea's outbound shipments of cosmetics jumped sharply from a year earlier in the first five months of the year, making beauty products the country's top consumer export item, Yonhap News Agency reported, citing government data. Combined outbound shipments of Korean beauty products came to US$5.6 billion during the January-May period, up from US$4.6 billion recorded a year earlier, according to data released on Sunday by the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Resources. Notably, cosmetics became the top export item among the country's five major consumer goods categories -- agricultural and fishery products, cosmetics, fashion apparel, household goods and pharmaceuticals.

items.title: Outgoing J-Kom chief Ismail Yusop thanks staff, says new responsibility awaits
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — Department of Community Communications (J-Kom) Director-General Datuk Ismail Yusop has announced the end of his service after more than a year leading the department, saying he will soon take on a new assignment. In a Facebook post, Ismail, affectionately known as “Tok Abah”, said every journey in life has its moments of meeting and parting, and that every ending opens the door to a new beginning. Mohd Khairuddin, also Selangor PKR secretary, was appointed J-Kom Director-General on March 1, 2023, replacing Prof Datuk Dr Mohammad Agus Yusof.

items.title: Pas to decide on Bersatu ties tomorrow, says Hadi
items.summary: MARANG: Pas will decide on its political cooperation with Bersatu tomorrow, says party president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang. He said the matter will be discussed at the Pas central committee meeting following deliberations by the party's Syura Council. "We will discuss and make a decision, especially regarding our relationship with Bersatu, at the Pas central committee meeting tomorrow," he told a press conference on Friday.

items.title: Anwar greenlights RM25m annual grant for AIMST, plus RM1m top-up for needy students
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, June 7 — The federal government has approved an additional RM1 million allocation for AIMST University to ensure students from underprivileged families continue to receive education assistance. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the additional allocation would supplement the annual grant given to the private university. Also present were Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir and MIC president Tan Sri Dr SA Vigneswaran, who is also AIMST University chancellor and chairman.At the ceremony, AIMST University received an annual grant amounting to RM25 million.

items.title: Anwar fields questions on AI, leadership, and education at AIMST University dialogue
items.summary: Speaking during the "Temu Anwar" programme at AIMST University, he addressed students' concerns on education access, artificial intelligence (AI), leadership and national unity, while stressing the importance of balancing academic excellence with humanity and compassion. "Universities must strive for excellence, but excellence alone is not enough. We need excellence with soul," he said.

items.title: Porsche crashes into Penang hotel lobby
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A 61-year-old woman lost control of her Porsche and crashed into a hotel lobby along Persiaran Gurney this afternoon. Northeast police chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said initial investigations indicate that the 11.15am incident occurred when the driver, who had just visited her family, mistakenly stepped on the accelerator while attempting to drive home. The extent of losses is still under investigation," Abdul Rozak said in a statement.

items.title: RM205m lifeline for MSMEs as Putrajaya unveils new business support initiatives
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 7 — The Ministry of Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development (Kuskop), through SME Corp Malaysia, has launched an industry and business support programme (Sinar) to assist micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs). Its minister, Steven Sim Chee Keong, said the Sinar programme offers financial assistance totalling RM205 million through four new initiatives for MSMEs nationwide to help expand their businesses. Elaborating further, he said Sinar is a new Kuskop programme implemented through SME Corp Malaysia, encompassing five new initiatives involving financial assistance.

items.title: GRS welcomes Upko's application to join coalition as positive development
items.summary: GRS secretary-general Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said Upko's bid reflected growing support for efforts to unite Sabah-based parties under a common platform. Armizan said Upko had formally submitted its application letter to GRS chairman Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor through party president Datuk Ewon Benedict and its senior leadership. Armizan said the key consideration would be Upko's commitment to GRS's four core principles — Sabah First, Sabah Forward, Sabah Prosper and Sabah United.

items.title: MIPP confirms BN invitation, to respond at 'appropriate time'
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) has confirmed receiving an invitation to join Barisan Nasional, though no decision has been made yet. MIPP president P. Punithan said the party is open to reviewing its future based on current political developments. Yesterday, FMT, quoting sources, reported that Ahmad Zahid and Punithan had held talks, and that MIPP was invited to join BN.

items.title: Survivor recalls horrific Gerik highway crash ahead of first anniversary
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH: "June 9 is a date I will remember for the rest of my life. These were the words of Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) student Abdul Wafiy Kamaruddin, 24, who survived the bus crash along the East-West Highway near Gerik, Perak, that claimed the lives of 15 students on June 9 last year. "I remember everything clearly.

items.title: CIMB sees Malaysia growing above 4.0pct despite Middle East risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is expected to sustain economic growth above four per cent this year despite escalating tensions in the Middle East, supported by resilient domestic demand, investment activity and electronics exports. "Growth troughs at 3.7 per cent in Q3 before recovering to 4.0 per cent in Q4," they said. A stronger-than-expected de-escalation could lift growth to 4.8 per cent, while a prolonged conflict coupled with weaker electronics demand may drag growth to 3.8 per cent.

items.title: Govt to scale up food rescue efforts under Ihsan Food Bank programme
items.summary: Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the initiative would serve as a guide for all stakeholders involved in carrying out the programme more effectively and systematically. “We intend to strengthen the i-FB programme so that we can develop and publish an action plan that will serve as a reference for all parties involved in implementing the initiative,” he told reporters after officiating the Ihsan Food Bank (i-FB) Initiative Programme here today. Armizan said the action plan would help align the direction and objectives of the programme while improving coordination among participating organisations and agencies.

items.title: ‘I can safely say 17 seats’ — Anthony Loke confirms DAP’s Johor allocation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — DAP is set to expand its footprint in the upcoming Johor state election, fielding candidates in 17 seats, an increase from the 14 it contested in the 2022 polls. “I can safely say that DAP will be contesting 17 seats in the coming state election in Johor,” Loke was quoted as saying in a Free Malaysia Today report. In the 2022 state election, DAP emerged as the coalition’s strongest performer in Johor, successfully defending 10 seats.

items.title: 'No such thing': Loke rules out PH-BN cooperation in Negri polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pakatan Harapan (PH) will not cooperate with Barisan Nasional (BN) in the upcoming Negri Sembilan election, DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said. Umno wants to contest all the seats in Johor, while PH wants to contest all the seats in Negri Sembilan. he said.

items.title: After 50 years of floods, govt plans RM80.5m project to make Teluk Intan flood-free by 2028
items.summary: TELUK INTAN, June 7 — Teluk Intan is expected to be declared a flood-free zone by 2028 through the implementation of the Phase Two Teluk Intan Flood Mitigation Plan (RTB), also known as the Teluk Intan Smart RTB project, which is currently in progress. “In less than 30 months, we will witness a new face of Teluk Intan. During the event, Nga also launched the Teluk Intan RTB Zone A Phase Two project, which commenced in December last year.

items.title: DAP to contest 17 seats in upcoming Johor election, says Loke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP will contest 17 seats in the upcoming Johor election, including the 14 seats it contested in 2022 and three additional constituencies. DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said DAP would defend the 10 seats it won in the last state election, as well as the four seats it contested but failed to capture, while seeking three more constituencies this time. "I can safely say that DAP will be contesting 17 seats in the coming state election in Johor," he told reporters.

items.title: Onn Hafiz: Johor gearing up for city-wide transformation ahead of RTS Link launch
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor caretaker government has unveiled five key measures aimed at ensuring the smooth implementation of the Johor Baru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link. Caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the state government was taking a comprehensive approach to prepare Johor Baru for the anticipated influx of commuters and the significant changes in traffic movement expected once the RTS Link goes into full operation on Jan 1 next year. This is expected to enhance the RTS Link and other public transport services integration by implementing smarter traffic management systems, expanding the park-and-ride concept, and reorganising traffic flow around key congestion hotspots.

items.title: Loke: Blaming everything on DAP is Rafizi's 'new syndrome'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke has hit back at Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli for claiming the party was behind the dissolution of the Negri Sembilan state assembly. Loke said the former PKR deputy president had developed a "new syndrome" of blaming DAP for everything. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Anwar to address Nikkei Forum, explore new investments in three-day visit to Japan
items.summary: TOKYO, June 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will undertake an official visit to Japan from June 8 to 10 at the invitation of the Japanese government, according to the Foreign Affairs Ministry. He will be accompanied by Foreign Affairs Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development Minister Steven Sim Chee Keong and senior government officials. “The Prime Minister will be accorded an official welcoming ceremony, followed by a bilateral meeting with his counterpart, Prime Minister of Japan Sanae Takaichi,” the ministry, widely known as Wisma Putra, said in a statement today.

items.title: Dubai luxury hotels woo staycationers as tourists flee
items.summary: DUBAI: Once reserved for wealthy travellers, Dubai's top-notch hotels have become almost exclusively reliant on residents, drawn in by dizzying staycation offers as war drives tourists away. On the Palm, an artificial island that has become synonymous with Dubai opulence, five-star hotels are busy on weekends and holidays once more, despite having been deserted by tourists. The clientele is driven by hotels offering residents-only deals that have become a lifeline for Dubai's luxury tourism.

items.title: Etihad Airways orders widebody planes, sees return to pre-war capacity in June
items.summary: RIO DE JANEIRO: Etihad Airways is ordering more widebody planes as the Middle East carrier expects to be flying about eight per cent more than a year ago by June 15, chief executive officer (CEO) Antonoaldo Neves said in an interview. Etihad is restoring flights after making cuts in March as the US-Israeli war on Iran turned regional, raising fuel prices, Neves said. He said Etihad does not plan to trim costs by cutting flights at the moment.

items.title: Body of elderly man found in drain, death ruled as drowning
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: The body of a 64-year-old man was found in a drain at Kampung Kolam, Jalan Merbau Kudung here yesterday, with an injury to his left ankle. Seberang Perai Utara district police chief Assistant Commissioner Anuar Abdul Rahman said police received a report on the discovery from a witness who was on his way to fish nearby. Anuar said the post-mortem found the cause of death to be drowning, with no criminal elements detected.

items.title: Fire destroys 80pct of Yong Peng MCA office, disrupts Johor election preparations [WATCH]
items.summary: MCA Youth chief Ling Tian Soon said the party is considering relocating its operations to temporary premises once investigations by the Fire and Rescue Department are completed. "This incident will affect MCA because the building has long served as the party's operations centre. Ling, who is also the Yong Peng assemblyman, said the blaze destroyed the building's public address system, newly installed air-conditioning units, furniture, and various documents and equipment used for community programmes.

items.title: Heavy rain, strong winds on the way: Thunderstorm warning in effect for Johor, Pahang, Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for several districts across Pahang, Johor, and Sarawak, cautioning residents to expect heavy rain, strong winds, and thunderstorms through 3 pm today. The warning, issued at 1:05 pm, covers the following areas: Pahang : Rompin Johor : Batu Pahat, Kluang, Pontian, Kulai, and Kota Tinggi Sarawak : Sri Aman; Betong (Pusa, Saratok, and Kabong); Sarikei; Mukah (Tanjung Manis, Daro, Matu, and Dalat); and Miri MetMalaysia triggers a thunderstorm warning when rainfall intensity is forecast to exceed 20 millimetres per hour, either imminently or for a sustained period of more than one hour. The warning remains in effect until 3:00 pm.

items.title: Body of 64-year-old man found in Kampung Kolam drain near Kepala Batas, cops say no foul play
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS, June 7 — The body of an elderly man was found in a drain at Kampung Kolam, Jalan Merbau Kudung here yesterday, with injuries to his left ankle. Seberang Perai Utara district police chief ACP Anuar Abdul Rahman said police received a report on the discovery of the 64-year-old man’s body from a man who was on his way to go fishing in the nearby area. “Investigations found that the victim was last seen by a witness about two days before the incident.

items.title: Police record statements from Jaslinda's rescuers
items.summary: TAPAH: Police have recorded statements from several Orang Asli villagers who rescued hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, after she went missing for 14 days in the Gunung Batu Putih area. District police chief Superintendent Abdul Malik Hasim said those questioned included Nazri Bah Eng, 51, and two friends who found the victim in a forested area near Kampung Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, yesterday. Malik said Jaslinda is still receiving treatment at Tapah Hospital after being brought down from the forest by rescuers yesterday evening.

items.title: Johor MCA seeks temporary base after fire destroys 80pc of Yong Peng office
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 7 — A fire that destroyed about 80 per cent of MCA’s office in Yong Peng yesterday has disrupted the party’s preparations for the upcoming Johor state election, its Youth chief Ling Tian Soon said. Ling said MCA is considering relocating its operations to a temporary premises after investigations by the Fire and Rescue Department are completed. The Yong Peng assemblyman said the blaze destroyed the building’s public address system, newly installed air-conditioning units, furniture, as well as documents and equipment used for community programmes.

items.title: Anwar heads to Japan for three-day visit, talks with Takaichi
items.summary: TOKYO: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will undertake a three-day official visit to Japan, during which he is scheduled to hold bilateral talks with his Japanese counterpart Sanae Takaichi. He is expected to open the conference's main session at 9.40am local time (8.40am Malaysian time). Among the regional leaders and dignitaries expected to attend are Timor-Leste President Jose Ramos-Horta, Laos Prime Minister Sonexay Siphandone, Thailand Deputy Prime Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow and Vietnam Deputy Prime Minister Le Tien Chau.

items.title: Police tracking suspects in viral MRR2 cable theft case
items.summary: AMPANG JAYA: Police are still tracking down a group of men believed to be involved in the cable theft along the Middle Ring Road 2 (MRR2) that went viral. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Khairul Anuar Khalid said investigations are ongoing and efforts to identify and locate the suspects are being intensified. Yesterday, it was reported that police were tracking down four men seen stealing utility cables along the MRR2 in a video that has gone viral.

items.title: Johor polls crucial for state's future direction and people's well-being, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The upcoming Johor election is an important platform to determine the state's future direction and the people's well-being. Pakatan Harapan (PH) communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said this message was conveyed during the launch of the coalition's election machinery for the state election in Batu Pahat last night, which was attended by more than 5,000 party workers and residents. The Johor State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 1 to pave the way for the state election.

items.title: Aminolhuda apologises for bury BN remark, says history shows defeat possible
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Pakatan Harapan chairman Aminolhuda Hassan has apologised for his controversial remark that the coalition would bury Barisan Nasional for a second time in Johor, but maintained that history has shown BN can be defeated at the polls. Aminolhuda said the comment was made out of shock after Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced that BN would contest all 56 state assembly seats independently despite being partners in the federal government. Aminolhuda said PH is confident facing the upcoming Johor election due to its strongholds and loyal voters, while fence-sitters will judge the current political stability, rapid economic recovery, and infrastructure development.

items.title: [UPDATED] Reject racism, religious bigotry, and corruption, Anwar tells youth
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Malaysian youth must reject racism, religious bigotry, and corruption to lead the nation forward with unity, conviction, and clarity of purpose. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the country's future depended on a new generation of Malaysians who remained united, focused, and determined to build a stronger nation. Enough of endemic corruption," he said.

items.title: ‘Johor has enormous potential’: Pakatan communications chief calls on poll campaigners to focus on development issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — The upcoming Johor state election is not merely a political contest but an important platform to determine the future direction of the state and the well-being of its people, said Pakatan Harapan (PH) Communications Director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. The Johor State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 1 to pave the way for the state election. — Bernama Pakatan Harapan communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil (left) speaks to Johor election campaign workers in Batu Pahat on June 6, 2026 in preparation for the coming state election.

items.title: [UPDATED] Don't be lulled into complacency despite strong economic performance, Anwar warns
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Malaysians should not be lulled into complacency despite strong economic indicators, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. He said education and integrity remained critical challenges facing the nation. Anwar said this was reflected in the government's budget priorities, with education receiving the largest allocation under his administration.

items.title: Anwar: Johor, Negri Sembilan politics must focus on justice, not racial divides
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Political contests in Johor and Negri Sembilan should centre on justice and equal opportunities, not racial rhetoric or divisive politics, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. The prime minister said political debates often became consumed by questions of race, status and wealth, while neglecting the future of ordinary Malaysians and their children. Anwar said true justice was not merely about providing equal treatment, but ensuring fair opportunities for those from disadvantaged backgrounds.

items.title: PN acknowledges Johor MB's strong image but eyes gains in next state polls
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Perikatan Nasional (PN) has acknowledged that Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi enjoys a strong public image and is widely seen as an effective administrator, but believes the coalition can increase its seat count in the next state election. State PN chairman Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal said while Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi is seen as active and performing well, a leader's individual popularity does not reflect the overall performance of the state government. Sahruddin said Bersatu will contest under the PN ticket alongside Pas, Gerakan, the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP), and Ikatan Prihatin Rakyat (IPR).

items.title: Google paying rival Musk US$920mil a month for data centre space
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: Elon Musk's space company SpaceX will collect US$920 million a month from Google in the coming years for renting its computer power to its giant tech competitor, reported German news agency dpa. In a similar deal, rival AI company Anthropic is also buying computing power from SpaceX for US$1.25 billion a month under a multi-year agreement. Musk folded his own AI company xAI into SpaceX and built several data centres for the firm.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim grants audience to caretaker Johor menteri besar at Istana Pasir Pelangi
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 7 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today granted an audience to Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi at Istana Pasir Pelangi here. According to a post on the official Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, His Majesty received a briefing on Johor's economic performance, development and progress. The briefing also covered efforts undertaken by the Johor state government to enhance the well-being of the people.

items.title: Highways clear this morning, but LLM warns of heavier traffic as holidaymakers return to Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — All highways heading towards the Klang Valley were experiencing smooth traffic flow this morning. He also advised motorists to plan their travel times to avoid getting caught in traffic jams. — Bernama

items.title: From ‘critical stage’ to miracle: The full 14-day survival story of Jaslinda Saludin (VIDEO)
items.summary: IPOH, June 7 — Woman hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who went missing for 14 days after she went on a hike at Gunung Batu Putih, near Tapah was found safe near Kampung Lubuk Geharu, Pos Musoh today by Orang Asli villager Nazri a/l Bah Eng at about 5 pm. May 23 Jaslinda, with 13 other people and two forestry mountain guides start their Trans Spencer Chapman expedition at 2 am on the Pos Gedung-Gunung Bah Gading-Gunung Batu Putih-Kuala Woh route. A search and rescue operation carries on looking for Jaslinda with a team of 12 Special Tactical Operation and Rescue Team (STORM) members dispatched to the peak of Gunung Batu Putih.

items.title: Former Kedah deputy speaker Datuk Mohd Jamil found dead in river near Alor Setar after disappearing overnight
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 7 — Former Kedah State Assembly deputy speaker Datuk Mohd Jamil Md Idross was found dead this morning after he was believed to have fallen into Sungai Teluk Bagan along Jalan Tandop here. Kota Setar police chief ACP Syed Basri Syed Ali said personnel from the Alor Setar Fire and Rescue Department discovered the 78-year-old’s body at about 8.10am. According to Syed Basri, family members last saw Mohd Jamil at about 11.30pm yesterday.

items.title: Pakatan completes Johor seat negotiations, shifts focus to election machinery preparation
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 7 — Seat negotiations among component parties within Pakatan Harapan (PH) for the 16th Johor State Election have been finalised, says PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. Thank you, Abang Mat Sabu (Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu), thank you Anthony Loke (DAP secretary-general),” he said while speaking at the launch of the PH election machinery for the Johor State Election, which was also attended by the Prime Minister and PH chairman, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said that with the seat negotiations completed, PH’s focus would now shift to election machinery preparations, including on-the-ground work, logistics coordination and delivering the right messages to voters to ensure PH’s victory in Johor.

items.title: ‘Don't treat people like slaves’ — Anwar vows end to degrading aid distribution practices
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reaffirmed the MADANI Government’s commitment to ending outdated practices that undermine the dignity of the people, including forcing those in need to queue for long hours to receive financial aid. Also present were DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke Siew Fook, PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu and PKR information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. Also in attendance were PKR vice-president and Johor PKR chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa, Johor Amanah chairman Aminolhuda Hassan and Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching.

items.title: Mosti monitoring Sabah airspace after reports of mysterious ‘bright light’ sighting
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 7 — The Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation (Mosti) has initiated a monitoring operation following reports of a mysterious bright light sighted in Sabah’s airspace earlier this week. In a statement today, the ministry confirmed that its Space Regulatory Authority Division (BPAngkasa) has received preliminary information regarding the phenomenon, which was reportedly observed by members of the public on the night of June 4. “Active monitoring is currently being carried out by BPAngkasa in cooperation with relevant agencies,” the statement read.

items.title: The cost of a heartbeat: How CVSKL Foundation is helping Malaysia’s sickest heart patients
items.summary: CVSKL Foundation chairman Tan Sri Rashpal Singh Randhay said the foundation was established as part of a broader commitment to ensure that financial hardship does not become a barrier to lifesaving care. We wanted to help patients who genuinely have no means of obtaining the treatment they need,” he said. Rashpal said the foundation’s assessment process is designed to ensure limited resources are directed to patients with the greatest need.

items.title: Batu Pahat MCA office gutted in evening fire, investigation underway (VIDEO)
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 7 — An MCA office in Jalan Nipah, Lorong 3, Yong Peng near here was 80 per cent destroyed in a fire last night. In a statement, operation commander Senior Fire Officer II Muhammad Amier Syah Hamzah said the Fire and Rescue Department received an emergency call on the incident at 8.28 pm. He said two Fire Rescue Tenders and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) unit, with a total of 14 personnel from the Yong Peng and Ayer Hitam fire stations, were deployed to the scene.

items.title: What’s really in Selangor’s guidelines for non-Muslim places of worship? Exco sets record straight
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 7 — The Selangor state government has clarified several misconceptions surrounding the proposed review and optimisation of the Selangor State Manual on Guidelines and Standards for Community Facilities Planning relating to non-Islamic places of worship. State Human Resources and Poverty Eradication Committee chairman V. Pappraidu said one of the inaccurate claims is that non-Islamic places of worship will no longer be allowed direct access from major roads, following the introduction of new guidelines requiring a service road. He said the provision of service roads is intended to facilitate safer vehicle access and parking without disrupting traffic flow on major roads.

items.title: ‘She was staggering and crying’: Village hero recounts finding missing hiker Jaslinda
items.summary: TAPAH, June 7 — Woman hiker, Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who went missing when hiking at Gunung Batu Putih on May 24, was found safe near Kampung Orang Asli Lubuk Gaharu yesterday. Villager Nazri a/l Bah Eng, 55, who discovered her at about 5 pm said she was staggering and holding a plastic bag containing several types of plants and mushrooms when he and two other family members were heading towards a river near their village. She was also carrying a plastic bag filled with mushrooms.

items.title: Missing hiker Jaslinda reveals how she survived two weeks in Tapah jungle without food or clean water (VIDEO)
items.summary: She shared how she scrounged up water from natural sources in the forest, including water from pitcher plants, to ensure her survival throughout her two-week ordeal. I drank water… dark water, brown water, water from pitcher plants. — Bernama https://www.facebook.com/bernamaofficial/videos/%F0%9D%97%A7%F0%9D%97%98%F0%9D%97%A5%F0%9D%97%9C%F0%9D%97%A0%F0%9D%97%94-%F0%9D%97%9E%F0%9D%97%94%F0%9D%97%A6%F0%9D%97%9C%F0%9D%97%9B-%F0%9D%97%A0%F0%9D%97%94%F0%9D%97%94%F0%9D%97%99-%F0%9D%97%A0%F0%9D%97%98%F0%9D%97%A1%F0%9D%97%AC%F0%9D%97%A8%F0%9D%97%A6%F0%9D%97%94%F0%9D%97%9B%F0%9D%97%9E%F0%9D%97%94%F0%9D%97%A1-%F0%9D%97%A5%F0%9D%97%94%F0%9D%97%9E%F0%9D%97%AC%F0%9D%97%94%F0%9D%97%A7-%F0%9D%97%A0%F0%9D%97%94%F0%9D%97%9F%F0%9D%97%94%F0%9D%97%AC%F0%9D%97%A6%F0%9D%97%9C%F0%9D%97%94-%F0%9D%97%9D%F0%9D%97%94%F0%9D%97%A6%F0%9D%97%9F%F0%9D%97%9C%F0%9D%97%A1%F0%9D%97%97%F0%9D%97%94pendaki-wanita-jaslinda-sa/2945268555669455/

items.title: Bomba says missing hiker Jaslinda fell multiple times during 14-day jungle ordeal
items.summary: TAPAH, June 7 — Hiker Jaslinda Saludin, who was found safe today after going missing for 14 days in the Gunung Batu Putih area here, sustained several minor injuries, including to her head, after falling multiple times while trying to find her way out of the forest. Based on information provided by Jaslinda to rescuers, she fell several times while navigating difficult terrain, including near river areas and from heights of about five and three metres, but managed to continue her journey each time. Earlier, Jaslinda was found by an Orang Asli resident near Kampung Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, at about 5 pm.

items.title: Johor polls: Rafizi rules out alliances, says Bersama ready to go solo
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 7 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) will not enter into negotiations with other political parties and will instead remain an independent contender in the upcoming Johor state election, says Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli. The party’s de facto leader said Bersama is steadfast in its decision to contest independently, despite the risk of losing its election deposits. He was accompanied by co-leader Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad and other party members.
